source_name: Code4rena Ethena Findings and Analysis Report (2023-10) - Low Risk and Non-Critical Issues
source_report_section: Low Risk and Non-Critical Issues / QA
issue_title: Easy DoS on big players when minting and redeeming in EthenaMinting.sol
summary: As indicated on the audit description, users intending to mint/redeem a large amount will need to mint/redeem over several blocks due to maxMintPerBlock or maxRedeemPerBlock. However, these RFQ’s are prone to DoS because mintedPerBlock[block.number] + mintAmount > maxMintPerBlock or redeemedPerBlock[block.number] + redeemAmount > maxRedeemPerBlock could revert by only 1 wei in excess.
root_cause: QA/low-risk implementation weakness described in the final C4 low/non-critical report.
why_not_medium_or_high: the issue appears in the final report low/non-critical or QA section and does not by itself prove a permissionless high-impact exploit
operational_risk: As indicated on the audit description, users intending to mint/redeem a large amount will need to mint/redeem over several blocks due to maxMintPerBlock or maxRedeemPerBlock. However, these RFQ’s are prone to DoS because mintedPerBlock[block.number] + mintAmount > maxMintPerBlock or redeemedPerBlock[block.number] + redeemAmount > maxRedeemPerBlock could revert by only 1 wei in excess.
when_it_could_escalate: if combined with permissionless access or large-value state transitions
if the affected path becomes critical to mint/redeem/withdrawal solvency or availability
